content.title: First Read Rule
body: If this is the agent's first time reading this README for the current work mission, read the entire README before opening deeper docs, following local routes, or relying on this corpus.

A first full pass is required once per current work mission, after a context reset, after a handoff or resume, or after the task materially changes. It is not required at every checkpoint in the same active mission.

After the first full pass, use the routing sections below to decide which local docs to reread or inspect next. At later checkpoints, reread only route-relevant sections unless the active `.core.*` route, roadmap, audit, or local stop condition requires a broader reread.
Do not treat this README as authority over App Behavior, architecture, implementation plans, proof, source, repo facts, product decisions, or explicit user instruction.
Purpose: Index for durable UX schema system decisions and rationale.
Audience: Agents and engineers evaluating or revising the UX schema system.
Keep In Sync With: UX schema files, model docs, examples, and open questions.
Update When: A schema decision changes, a new durable decision is made, or rationale becomes stale.
Does Not Cover: Detailed field-by-field schema reference or generator implementation.

content.title: Decisions
body: This folder records why the UX schema system exists, what it governs, what it excludes, and which decisions should remain stable unless intentionally revised.
Files are prefixed in recommended reading order.

content.title: Routing
body: Use this area when a UX-system decision record may govern schema, model, generation, storage, or App Behavior artifact handling.
Does not own new product behavior by itself; it records durable UX-system decisions that must still be applied within their stated authority boundary.
Stop when the agent can state the relevant decision record, what it governs, what it does not govern, and which downstream README or artifact should be read next.
